protocol.name: blur
protocol.copyright:
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2015 Martin Gräßlin SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2015 Marco Martin
SPDX-License-Identifier: LGPL-2.1-or-later

interface.name: org_kde_kwin_blur_manager
interface.description: This interface allows a client to request blur effect for a surface.

request.name: create

description.summary: create a blur object for a surface

request.description: Create a blur object for a surface.

request.name: unset
request.description: Remove blur effect from a surface.

interface.name: org_kde_kwin_blur
interface.description: This interface allows a client to specify a blur region for a surface.

request.name: commit
request.description: Apply all the previously configured state for this blur object.

request.name: set_region
request.description: Set the blur region. If region is null, the entire surface will be blurred.

request.name: release
request.description: Release the blur object, removing the blur effect from the surface.

request.name: set_radius
request.description: Set the blur radius in pixels. If not set, the compositor's default blur radius is used. The value is a fixed-point number with 8 bits of fractional precision (i.e. multiply the desired radius by 256). For example, a 2px blur is 512, a 60px blur is 15360.

request.name: set_saturation
request.description: Set the saturation applied to the blurred backdrop, matching CSS `backdrop-filter: saturate(x)`. 1.0 leaves saturation unchanged, values above 1.0 increase it, below 1.0 desaturate. If not set, the compositor applies no saturation change. The value is a fixed-point number with 8 bits of fractional precision (i.e. multiply the desired multiplier by 256). For example, saturate(1.0) is 256, saturate(1.3) is 332, saturate(2.0) is 512.

request.name: set_tint
request.description: Set how strongly the compositor blends a white overlay onto the blurred backdrop (the "frosted glass" lightening). 0.0 disables the tint so the backdrop is a faithful `backdrop-filter: blur()` and the surface's own background provides the glass colour; higher values add more white. If not set, the compositor default tint is used. The value is a fixed-point number with 8 bits of fractional precision (multiply by 256). For example, 0.0 is 0, the default 0.15 is 38, full white is 256.

request.name: set_border
request.description: Set the alpha of the compositor's 1px frosted-glass border drawn around the blurred backdrop. 0.0 disables the border entirely (useful when the surface draws its own border client-side); the compositor default is roughly 0.2. If not set, the compositor default border is used. The value is a fixed-point number with 8 bits of fractional precision (multiply by 256). For example, 0.0 is 0, 0.2 is 51.
